input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.

delta_p.trigger: If summary_1 lists two or more candidate novel titles and at least one of those titles is shown with an explicit year (e.g. a parenthetical year) while at least one other title in summary_1 lacks any year marker.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep all base-prompt requirements: emit exactly one compact, keyword-style retrieval query string (single line), anchor the query to visible tokens in summary_1, include the author token 'Sir Arthur Conan Doyle' if it already appears in the visible inputs, and do not add explanatory text or extra fields.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, prefer the candidate title from summary_1 that does NOT already display a year. Construct the query as: <UnyearedTitle> publication year Sir Arthur Conan Doyle (or if the author token is not present in summary_1, use: <UnyearedTitle> publication year). Use only tokens exactly as they appear in summary_1 and keep the query compact (keywords only).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not emit a query targeting a title that already includes a parenthetical year in summary_1. Do not introduce any new proper names or anchors that do not appear in the visible inputs. Do not output multiple queries, explanatory text, or attempts to copy the target answer.
delta_p.output_contract: Return exactly one single-line string in the 'query' field containing the compact keyword-style search; no other fields, punctuation, or commentary.
delta_p.why_local_transition: Shifts the retrieval focus away from a title already resolved in the visible summary toward the unresolved candidate title, so the downstream retriever can collect the missing publication-year evidence for that other novel.

delta_p.trigger: If summary_1 lists more than one candidate Sherlock Holmes novel or explicitly signals ambiguity about which "crime novel" the question names.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing requirement to emit exactly one compact, single-line retrieval query and continue to anchor the query on tokens that appear in summary_1.
delta_p.change_rule: When ambiguity is present, build the query from the explicit novel title(s shown in summary_1 plus concise relation keywords for date (for example: publication year, written, publication date) and the author name 'Sir Arthur Conan Doyle'. Prefer phrasing like: <VisibleTitle> publication year Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. If summary_1 shows multiple candidate titles, include both visible titles followed by the relation keywords (e.g., <TitleA> <TitleB> publication year Sir Arthur Conan Doyle) so the retrieval can disambiguate which novel and its year.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not add any proper names, supporting-person names, or other anchors that are not present in summary_1. Do not use full-sentence questions, explanatory text, or speculative assertions. Do not include punctuation or extra fields; emit only the single compact query string.
delta_p.output_contract: Return exactly one single-line retrieval query string and nothing else. The string must use only anchors visible in summary_1 and concise relation keywords to locate the novel's writing/publication year.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The source query used an unsupported extra anchor and thus risked retrieving irrelevant material; this delta narrows behavior to use only visible candidate titles plus date-related keywords so the second-hop retrieval will recover the missing publication/writing year needed to resolve the ambiguity.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What year was the crime novel written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle written, featuring Sherlock Holmes?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1: Passages identify two Doyle Holmes novels: Passage 1 names "The Sign of the Four" as "the second novel featuring Sherlock Holmes written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle" (the passage also shows a parenthetical year next to that title), and Passage 3 names "The Hound of the Baskervilles" as "the third of four Sherlock Holmes novels written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle." Missing/next-hop: determine which specific "crime novel" the question refers to (which title is meant) and then retrieve that novel's publication/writing year (if the intended novel is "The Sign of the Four," confirm the parenthetical year shown in Passage 1 as the publication year).

current_output.query: The Sign of the Four publication year Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

input_dict.output_requirement: Return exactly {"query": "<one compact second-hop BM25 query>"}. Do not return explanations or multiple queries.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
query

{
  "question": "What year was the crime novel written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle written, featuring Sherlock Holmes?",
  "summary_1": "Passages identify two Doyle Holmes novels: Passage 1 names \"The Sign of the Four\" as \"the second novel featuring Sherlock Holmes written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle\" (the passage also shows a parenthetical year next to that title), and Passage 3 names \"The Hound of the Baskervilles\" as \"the third of four Sherlock Holmes novels written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle.\" Missing/next-hop: determine which specific \"crime novel\" the question refers to (which title is meant) and then retrieve that novel's publication/writing year (if the intended novel is \"The Sign of the Four,\" confirm the parenthetical year shown in Passage 1 as the publication year).",
